response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and distributed systems engineer with a broad polyglot background, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The most critical disqualifier is the near-total absence of C++ experience: the role mandates 8 years minimum, yet C++ does not appear in his skills section and surfaces only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project. His primary expertise lies in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web-layer distributed systems — not cloud compute infrastructure or systems-level programming. He also lacks documented technical leadership of cloud or infrastructure teams, and holds no advanced degree. No amount of breadth compensates for missing the hard minimum on the primary technical requirement.
strengths: Meaningful distributed systems exposure at scale (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O, high-performance databases)
Consistent 'Staff' engineer trajectory with evidence of knowledge-sharing, mentoring, and community contribution (workshops, talks, open-source projects)
Hands-on infrastructure and DevOps experience: Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, AWS CDK
concerns: Virtually no C++ experience — the role's single most critical hard requirement (8 years minimum) is almost entirely unmet
No cloud compute platform or systems-level infrastructure background; experience is web-application and micro-services oriented
No documented technical leadership of cloud or infrastructure engineering teams; 'Staff' titles reflect individual seniority, not team-level technical leadership
